item.title: LLM Hosting Options for Regulated Industries
item.description: From public SaaS to air-gapped on-prem: a practical ladder of LLM hosting options, and how to choose based on regulatory pressure and engineering maturity.
item.content:encoded: From public SaaS to air-gapped on-prem: a practical ladder of LLM hosting options, and how to choose based on regulatory pressure and engineering maturity.

item.title: HTTPS Encryption Lifecycle - Under the Hood
item.description: One HTTPS call from TCP open through TLS handshake, session keys, AEAD records, and teardown. Where asymmetric trust ends and symmetric bulk encryption begins.
item.content:encoded: One HTTPS call from TCP open through TLS handshake, session keys, AEAD records, and teardown. Where asymmetric trust ends and symmetric bulk encryption begins.

item.title: HTTP vs HTTPS - Under the Hood
item.description: HTTP is the application protocol for requests and responses. HTTPS is HTTP over TLS. Methods, status, headers, the TLS wrap, certificates, and which failures people mislabel as network or app.
item.content:encoded: HTTP is the application protocol for requests and responses. HTTPS is HTTP over TLS. Methods, status, headers, the TLS wrap, certificates, and which failures people mislabel as network or app.

item.title: Symmetric vs Asymmetric Encryption - Under the Hood
item.description: Symmetric crypto shares one secret for speed. Asymmetric uses key pairs for trust and key exchange. Where each appears in TLS, APIs, data at rest, and which mistakes look like security.
item.content:encoded: Symmetric crypto shares one secret for speed. Asymmetric uses key pairs for trust and key exchange. Where each appears in TLS, APIs, data at rest, and which mistakes look like security.

item.title: Anycast vs Unicast - Under the Hood
item.description: Unicast maps one IP to one place. Anycast maps one IP to many places and lets BGP pick the path. How both work, where DNS and CDNs use them, and which failures look like app bugs.
item.content:encoded: Unicast maps one IP to one place. Anycast maps one IP to many places and lets BGP pick the path. How both work, where DNS and CDNs use them, and which failures look like app bugs.

item.title: TCP vs UDP - Under the Hood
item.description: TCP is a reliable byte stream with handshake, ACKs, and congestion control. UDP is datagrams with no delivery guarantee. Components, steps, use cases, and which failures people mislabel as app bugs.
item.content:encoded: TCP is a reliable byte stream with handshake, ACKs, and congestion control. UDP is datagrams with no delivery guarantee. Components, steps, use cases, and which failures people mislabel as app bugs.

item.title: CDN - Under the Hood
item.description: A CDN is a cache-and-route fabric at the edge: PoPs, origin, cache keys, TTL, anycast, and the hit/miss path. Why delivery scales, and which failures people mislabel as app or network.
item.content:encoded: A CDN is a cache-and-route fabric at the edge: PoPs, origin, cache keys, TTL, anycast, and the hit/miss path. Why delivery scales, and which failures people mislabel as app or network.

item.title: DNS - Under the Hood
item.description: DNS is a globally distributed, hierarchical, eventually consistent naming database. Stub, recursive resolver, root, TLD, authoritative name server, caching, TTL, and anycast.
item.content:encoded: DNS is a globally distributed, hierarchical, eventually consistent naming database. Stub, recursive resolver, root, TLD, authoritative name server, caching, TTL, and anycast.

item.title: The Blueprint Before Training an LLM
item.description: Hyperparameters, vocabulary, and why a 7B model is 7B before day one, how the dials connect, where parameters live, and why the context window is a budgeted choice.
item.content:encoded: Hyperparameters, vocabulary, and why a 7B model is 7B before day one, how the dials connect, where parameters live, and why the context window is a budgeted choice.

item.title: Is MCP Really Necessary for Business Agents in Large Regulated Enterprises?
item.description: MCP is a valuable standard for AI tool interoperability. For regulated business agents, the question is whether its benefits outweigh operational cost for your use case, not whether MCP is good.
item.content:encoded: MCP is a valuable standard for AI tool interoperability. For regulated business agents, the question is whether its benefits outweigh operational cost for your use case, not whether MCP is good.

item.title: Retrieval Is a Governed Action
item.description: Retrieval is not a database query; it is a governed action. How Policy-Governed Agent Runtime applies to RAG when context construction must be scoped, auditable, and enforced before inference.
item.content:encoded: Retrieval is not a database query; it is a governed action. How Policy-Governed Agent Runtime applies to RAG when context construction must be scoped, auditable, and enforced before inference.

item.title: How to Design an Intent Router for Agentic AI
item.description: A practical design guide for intent routing — route tables, layered classification, confidence thresholds, session stickiness, eval gates, and wiring dispatch into the agentic app before the model loop runs.
item.content:encoded: A practical design guide for intent routing — route tables, layered classification, confidence thresholds, session stickiness, eval gates, and wiring dispatch into the agentic app before the model loop runs.

item.title: What Is an Intent Router — and Why It Matters in Agentic AI
item.description: An intent router is the first deterministic decision in an agent stack. It maps user requests to the right workflow, agent, and tool manifest before the model loop runs — and when it fails, every downstream stage can execute perfectly and still miss the user’s goal.
item.content:encoded: An intent router is the first deterministic decision in an agent stack. It maps user requests to the right workflow, agent, and tool manifest before the model loop runs — and when it fails, every downstream stage can execute perfectly and still miss the user’s goal.

item.title: RAG Is Not a Database
item.description: RAG is runtime context construction at query time, not a storage layer you bolt onto an LLM.
item.content:encoded: RAG is runtime context construction at query time, not a storage layer you bolt onto an LLM.

item.title: Policy-Governed Agent Runtime
item.description: Proposal is not permission. Agents propose tool calls; governance decides whether they run. An architecture breakdown of runtime trust boundaries for production agent systems in regulated industries.
item.content:encoded: Proposal is not permission. Agents propose tool calls; governance decides whether they run. An architecture breakdown of runtime trust boundaries for production agent systems in regulated industries.

item.title: AI Observability In Enterprise
item.description: AI observability is not a dashboard. It is a capture-and-retention architecture with five signals, five retention policies, and four consumers.
item.content:encoded: AI observability is not a dashboard. It is a capture-and-retention architecture with five signals, five retention policies, and four consumers.

item.title: Hallucinations Is a System Design Problem
item.description: Hallucination is not the model failing. It is the model succeeding at the wrong objective in a system that never gave it the right one.
item.content:encoded: Hallucination is not the model failing. It is the model succeeding at the wrong objective in a system that never gave it the right one.

item.title: How LLM Works Under the Hood
item.description: A 20,000-ft view of the LLM lifecycle and why understanding the four stages matters for enterprise architecture.
item.content:encoded: A 20,000-ft view of the LLM lifecycle and why understanding the four stages matters for enterprise architecture.
